questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The solution of the equation $|x-1|^{\log _3 x^2-2 \log _8 9}=(x-1)^7$ is/are
questions.solution:
$\because x-1\gt0$
Taking log both sides $\left(\log _3 x^2-2 \log _x 9\right) \log (x-1)=7 \log (x-1)$
$\Rightarrow x=81, \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}, 2$
$\therefore x \neq \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$
$\therefore x=2,81$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The solution(s) of the equation $2 \log _3(x-2)+\log _3(x-4)^2=0$, is/are
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \log ((x-2)|x-4|)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow(x-2)|x-4|=1\end{aligned}$
Case (I): $x-4\gt0$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow(x-2)(x-4)=1 \\ & \Rightarrow x=3 \pm \sqrt{2} \\ & \because x \neq 3-\sqrt{2} \\ & \therefore x=3+\sqrt{2}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Case (II): If, } x-4 \lt 0 \\ & \Rightarrow-(x-2)(x-4)=1 \\ & \Rightarrow x=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Solution(s) of the equation $x^{\left(\frac{\log _{10} x+7}{4}\right)}=10^{\log _{10} x+1}$, is/are
questions.solution:
$x^{\left(\frac{\log _{10} x+7}{4}\right)}=10^{\log _{10} x+1}$
Taking log both sides on base, we get
$\left(\frac{\log _{10} x+7}{4}\right) \log _{10} x=\left(\log _{10} x+1\right) \log _{10} 10$
Put, $\log _{10} x=1$
$\therefore(t+7) t=4 t+4$
$\Rightarrow t=-4,1$
$x=10^{-1}, 10$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $x$ and $y$ are positive real number such that $\log _9 x+\log _{27} y=\frac{7}{2}$ and $\log _{27} x+\log _9 y=\frac{2}{3}$, then:
questions.solution:
Let $\log _3 x=A \quad$ and $\quad \log _3 y=B$
$\begin{array}{llll}\therefore & \frac{A}{2}+\frac{B}{3}=\frac{7}{2} & \Rightarrow & 3 A+2 B=21 \\ \text { and } & \frac{A}{3}+\frac{B}{2}=\frac{2}{3} & \Rightarrow & 2 A+3 B=4 \\ \therefore & A=\log _3 x=11 & \Rightarrow & x=3^{11} \\ \text { and } & B=\log _3 y=-6 & \Rightarrow & y=3^{-6}\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let equation $x^{\log _2 x-4}=32$ has two real solutions $x_1$ and $x_2\left(x_1>x_2\right)$, then which of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
$\left(\log _2 x-4\right) \cdot \log _2 x=5$
Let $\log _2 x=a$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & a^2-4 a-5=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad(a-5)(a+1)=0 \\ \Rightarrow & a=5 \text { or } a=-1 \Rightarrow \quad x=32 \text { or } x=\frac{1}{2}\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $y_i(i=1$ to $n)$ are the solution satisfying the equation $y^{\log _3(\sqrt{3 y})}=y^{\log _3(3 y)}-6$ where $y>0$. Then:
questions.solution:
Let $\quad y^{\log _3(\sqrt{3 y})}=t$
$\begin{aligned} & t=t^2-6 \quad \Rightarrow \quad t^2-t-6=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad(t-3)(t+2)=0 \\ & t=3\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad & y^{\log _3(\sqrt{3 y})}=3 \\ & \frac{1}{2} \log _3 3 y \cdot \log _e y=1\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2}(1+a) a=1 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a^2+a-2=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad(a+2)(a-1)=0 \\ & \begin{array}{l}a=1,-2 \\ y=3,1 / 9 \\ y_1=1 / 9 \quad \text { and } y_2=3\end{array}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following is NOT in the solution set of the inequality $\left|x^2-1\right|+\left|2 x^2-3\right| \lt \left|2-x^2\right|$ ?
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& 1-\log _{3^2}(x+1)^2=\frac{1}{2} \log _{3^{1 / 2}}\left(\frac{x+5}{x+3}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow 1-\frac{2}{2} \log _3|x+1|=\frac{2}{2} \log _3\left(\frac{x+5}{x+3}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \log _3\left(\frac{3}{|x+1|}\right)=\log _3\left(\frac{x+5}{x+3}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{3}{|x+1|}=\frac{x+5}{x+3} \\
& \text { Case- I } \qquad x+1\gt0 \Rightarrow x\gt-1 \\
& \Rightarrow 3(x+3)=(x+1)(x+5) \\
& \Rightarrow x^2+3 x-4=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x=-4 \text { or } x=1 \\
& x=-4 \text { rejected } \qquad (\because x\gt-1) \\
& \therefore \mathrm{x}=1 \\
& \text { Case - II } \qquad \mathrm{x}+1 \lt 0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{x} \lt -1 \\
& \Rightarrow 3(x+3)=-(x+1)(x+5) \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}^2+9 \mathrm{x}+14=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x=-2 \text { or } x=-7 \\
& \therefore \text { Set of value of } x=\{-7,-2,1\}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
